key_insight: A lightweight MobileNetV3 classifier can accurately detect multiple pipeline weld defect classes from noisy RGB images, making automated inspection practical on-device.
engineering_relevance: The approach supports faster, more consistent pipeline quality control with low computational cost, which is valuable for handheld and mobile inspection workflows.
method: Preprocess and augment weld images to improve robustness to noise and variable acquisition conditions.
Train and compare MobileNetV3 Small and Large for multi-class defect classification.
Validate on two practical datasets collected with HDR cameras and a mobile phone in uncontrolled settings.
evidence_signal: The study reports 99.99% accuracy on the 4,927-image AI5083 dataset and improved performance on the smaller PWDID dataset, with about a 3% gain over prior methods.
watch_item: The very high reported performance may not fully transfer to real deployment unless class imbalance, domain shift, and low-quality field imagery are carefully handled.
paper_title: MobileNetV3 for Pipeline Welding Defect Detection: A Computer Vision Approach on Noisy RGB Images
